home.welcome-text:
I'm Liv Audigane, a fullstack engineer and former journalist with {{profile.experience}} years of professional experience across different fields. With {{profile.techExperience}} years in software development, I'm currently at L'Express working across the full stack (React, PHP, Symfony, TypeScript, Twig) on an editorial website serving 300,000 daily readers, with responsibilities ranging from feature development to technical scoping and documentation. I also mentor junior developers. Before that, I spent two years at CGI on a Java/Angular project for Les Mousquetaires, dealing with large-scale performance constraints (including database tables of over 6 million rows). 
 
 Seven years of journalism specialising in science, society and technology, including nearly three years at BFMTV.com, taught me to put context and impact at the centre of my work. A habit I bring to my code too. 
 
 I hold a Master's in Journalism and Political Science and a BA in Applied Modern Languages. I'm fluent in English (C2) and have studied German, Russian, and Spanish. I've studied in international environments and adapt quickly to new contexts. Outside of work, I volunteer with the Croix-Rouge Française in first aid and social welfare. 
 
 If you're looking for someone who can build, document, mentor, and communicate, I'd love to connect.
